heading: Nonreduction in pay while serving in the uniformed services or National Guard
label: 5538 Nonreduction in pay while serving in the uniformed services or National Guard
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). inserted “ or” after “under” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–232section 12304b of title 102009—Subsec. (b). added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b), which read as follows:Pub. L. 111–117“(b)(1) Amounts under this section shall be payable with respect to each pay period (which would otherwise apply if the employee’s civilian employment had not been interrupted)—“(A) during which such employee is entitled to reemployment rights under chapter 43 of title 38 with respect to the position from which such employee is absent (as referred to in subsection (a)); and“(B) for which such employee does not otherwise receive basic pay (including by taking any annual, military, or other paid leave) to which such employee is entitled by virtue of such employee’s civilian employment with the Government.“(2) For purposes of this section, the period during which an employee is entitled to reemployment rights under chapter 43 of title 38—“(A) shall be determined disregarding the provisions of ; andsection 4312(d) of title 38“(B) shall include any period of time specified in within which an employee may report or apply for employment or reemployment following completion of service on active duty to which called or ordered as described in subsection (a).”section 4312(e) of title 38

children.text: the amount of basic pay which would otherwise have been payable to such employee for such pay period if such employee’s civilian employment with the Government had not been interrupted by that service, exceeds (if at all)

children.label: A
children.text: is payable to such employee for that service; and

children.label: B
children.text: is allocable to such pay period.
during which such employee is entitled to re-employment rights under chapter 43 of title 38 with respect to the position from which such employee is absent (as referred to in subsection (a)); and

for which such employee does not otherwise receive basic pay (including by taking any annual, military, or other paid leave) to which such employee is entitled by virtue of such employee’s civilian employment with the Government.
from the appropriation or fund which would be used to pay the employee if such employee were in a pay status; and
to the extent practicable, at the same time and in the same manner as would basic pay if such employee’s civilian employment had not been interrupted.

children.label: d
children.text: The Office of Personnel Management shall, in consultation with Secretary of Defense, prescribe any regulations necessary to carry out the preceding provisions of this section.

The head of each agency referred to in section 2302(a)(2)(C)(ii) shall, in consultation with the Office, prescribe procedures to ensure that the rights under this section apply to the employees of such agency.

The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration shall, in consultation with the Office, prescribe procedures to ensure that the rights under this section apply to the employees of that agency.
the terms “employee”, “Federal Government”, and “uniformed services” have the same respective meanings as given those terms in ;section 4303 of title 38

the term “employing agency”, as used with respect to an employee entitled to any payments under this section, means the agency or other entity of the Government (including an agency referred to in section 2302(a)(2)(C)(ii)) with respect to which such employee has reemployment rights under chapter 43 of title 38; and
the term “basic pay” includes any amount payable under section 5304.
